new_classes.label_en: Other sparkling wine production region (not Champagne, not Prosecco)
new_classes.definition_en: Any geographic site of sparkling wine production that is neither the Champagne AOC region (France) nor the Prosecco DOC/DOCG region (Italy); used to delimit the geographic scope for CN 2204 10 94.

A process of producing sparkling wine from fresh grapes that occurs in a geographic site other than the Champagne AOC region or the Prosecco DOC/DOCG region; corresponds to the residual 'other' category within CN subheading 2204 10 (sparkling wine).

Sparkling wine of fresh grapes (CN heading 2204 10) that is neither Champagne (CN 2204 10 11) nor Prosecco (CN 2204 10 15); the residual 'other' subheading within sparkling wines under CN 2204 10 as referenced in subheading note 1 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for 22041011–22041098 simply states "Sparkling wine – See subheading note 1 to this chapter." CN 2204 10 94 is the residual 'other' category for sparkling wines not falling under 2204 10 11 (Champagne) or 2204 10 15 (Prosecco). The proposed axioms capture: (1) the sparkling-wine quality (carbonation/excess pressure), (2) the 'other region' geographic restriction via the BFO:Site + occurs_in + producedBy pattern, and (3) explicit complement restrictions excluding Champagne and Prosecco. A minor gap is that the full content of subheading note 1 (which specifies excess-pressure thresholds for sparkling wine, e.g. ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C) is only partially encoded via the existing SparklingWineQuality class rather than an explicit numeric pressure restriction, since the precise pressure facets are carried by that quality class in the broader TBox. Additionally, any further named sparkling wine appellations that may be enumerated between 2204 10 15 and 2204 10 94 in the current CN are not individually referenced, since only Champagne (11) and Prosecco (15) appear in the running TBox.
